subject: Programming, DS & Algorithms
question: Binary search requires:
explanation: Binary search needs sorted data to decide which half to search.

subject: Programming, DS & Algorithms
question: Time complexity of binary search:
explanation: Halves search space each step: log₂(n) comparisons maximum.

subject: Programming, DS & Algorithms
question: Binary search on 1024 elements needs at most:
explanation: log₂(1024) = 10. At most 10 comparisons.

subject: Programming, DS & Algorithms
question: Lower bound of comparison-based search is:
explanation: Information-theoretic lower bound: any comparison-based search needs Ω(log n) in worst case.

subject: Programming, DS & Algorithms
question: Binary search returns -1 when:
explanation: Convention: return -1 (or NOT_FOUND) when target doesn't exist in array.
